key_points: Electric circuits must form a continuous conducting path between the positive and negative terminals of a power source
Circuit diagrams represent electric circuits using standardized symbols
Different electrical components perform specific functions such as supplying energy, controlling current and converting energy into light or heat
Multiple components can be connected in series or parallel depending on the circuit design
Safety and proper insulation are important when working with electrical circuits
examples: Series circuit: Two bulbs connected in a single path share the same current
Parallel circuit: Bulbs connected in separate branches operate independently
Circuit diagram symbols: Cell (long and short lines), bulb (circle with filament), switch (break in line)
Conductivity test: A simple tester circuit used to check whether a material allows current to pass
formulas: Electric circuit → Continuous closed path for electric current
Series connection → Components connected one after another in a single path
Parallel connection → Components connected in separate branches across the same source
exam_tips: Memorize standard electrical symbols used in circuit diagrams
Understand the difference between series and parallel circuits
Practice drawing labelled circuit diagrams clearly
Carefully analyze why a device works or fails in a circuit problem
